creator: raimeetravel
caption:
This might actually be one of the best quick international getaways for Americans right now 🇨🇦 #sponsored

Calgary is less than 4 hours from a LOT of major U.S. airports, the American dollar is super strong right now, and you get this mix of amazing food, arts, city life, and then literal world-class nature right nearby.

You can spend the morning getting coffee + amazing food in Calgary and then be surrounded by mountains in Banff a couple hours later.

A few things I loved:

• incredible restaurant scene
• cool bars + live music spots
• easy access from the US
• skiing + hiking nearby depending on season
• some of the prettiest scenery I’ve seen in North America

It’s a trip that feels big but isn’t complicated! No long haul flights, no currency stress, just a really good time!!! Comment ALBERTA and I’ll send you more info!

#ExploreAlberta #ExploreCanada #CanadaTravel #Calgary BanffNationalPark

transcription.text: i stand by the fact that you do not need to fly overseas or spend eight plus hours in a plane for a good international trip. this is one of the best ones you can take and it's less than four hours from most major u.s airports. and as an american you don't need a visa, there's no jet lag and you can go summer skiing or snowboarding here. it's calgary and banff in alberta canada and this is how you can spend the perfect long weekend there. fly in on a friday, one full day in the vibrant city of calgary that has an incredible food and wellness scene, then saturday morning you'll drive 90 minutes and everything changes. you're now going to be in the middle of the canadian rockies and downtown banff is super cute too with amazing food and great vibes. then sunday you'll hit up another resort. there's multiple world-class resorts here to choose from and terrain for every level. then monday you'll drive back, explore the city more and you can fly back home that night and be ready for work on tuesday. these were some of my favorite activities that you can spread out during your time in calgary and some of my favorite restaurants in both calgary and banff. if you've been waiting for a sign to finally book an international trip, this is it. it's the easiest long weekend you'll ever plan.
